intro: Pyrus communis, commonly referred to as Pyrus ignava, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the Rosaceae family, known for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and fruit of Pyrus communis are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include quercetin, rutin, and pyrogallol, which have been shown to possess protective effects against cardiovascular diseases, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders. Pyrus communis is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
